immutable_core.semantic_definition: A parent microbiology laboratory result event at specimen-test grain, retaining specimen/test context, event timing, result-comment inference helpers, and child organism/susceptibility counts without flattening child entities into the parent row.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per microbiology specimen-test event
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_microbiology_test_event must preserve parent specimen-test event semantics and must not silently become an organism isolate, susceptibility leaf, microbiology order, antibiotic treatment event, suspected-infection phenotype, Sepsis-3 onset, or organism ontology table.

microbiology_entity_contract.entity_grain: one row per microbiology specimen-test event
microbiology_entity_contract.source_hierarchy_rule: retain performed microbiology laboratory result events from the source hierarchy and expose child organism/susceptibility counts rather than flattening children into the event row
microbiology_entity_contract.parent_child_rule: this entity is the parent/root row; child organism branches link by microbiology_test_event_id
microbiology_entity_contract.raw_text_retention_rule: retain raw specimen, test, comment, and Gram-stain text fields needed for source-faithful interpretation
microbiology_entity_contract.no_row_interpretation: no retained event row means no approved opening-scope performed microbiology result event under this mapping, not absence of culture order, infection, organism, or antimicrobial exposure

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_microbiology_test_event outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
